source_record.title: CD4+ CAR T-cell exhaustion associated with early relapse of multiple myeloma after BCMA CAR T-cell therapy.
source_record.study_context: Multiple myeloma after BCMA CAR T-cell therapy; PubMed abstract and metadata used for this v0 public extraction.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports greater CAR T-cell persistence in patients with durable response than in patients with transient response.
measurement_fields: transient versus durable clinical response group
limitations: This extraction uses public abstract and metadata, not full paper-level reanalysis.
Association does not establish persistence as the only or sufficient cause of durable response.
Product, patient, and disease-state details require full-text review.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports increased cytotoxic CD4+ CAR T cells with exhaustion markers in short-lived response contexts.
limitations: Marker association does not by itself prove causality.
The public extraction does not reproduce single-cell analyses.
The signal should be reviewed with full methods and cohort context.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The source reports enrichment of nonclassical monocytes in the myeloma niche and proposes CAR T-cell dysfunction mechanisms involving transforming growth factor beta.
limitations: The v0 extraction records the proposed mechanism signal without quantifying effect size.
Microenvironment signals may interact with product, disease, and patient context.
Requires expert review before being used in educational summaries.

extracted_signals.signal_summary: The public abstract exposes several measurement concepts but not enough detail to standardize extraction without full-text review.
limitations: Public abstract and metadata are not enough for a complete structured evidence review.
Full text and methods are needed before quantitative comparison with other sources.

what_this_supports: CAR T-cell persistence and exhaustion-related T-cell states belong in the post-CAR T relapse mechanism extraction template.
Public extractions should capture immune-cell phenotype, persistence, and measurement timing fields.
Microenvironment-linked immune dysfunction should be tracked as a candidate signal under CAR T fitness and persistence until a dedicated bucket is warranted.
what_this_does_not_support: It does not prove that CD4+ CAR T-cell exhaustion is the only relapse mechanism.
It does not estimate relapse frequency across all BCMA CAR T products.
It does not recommend treatment sequencing or trial selection.
It does not establish patient-specific prognosis.

review_notes: Built from PubMed ESummary and EFetch metadata/abstract for PMID 38574299.
This example demonstrates extraction shape and should be expanded after full-text expert review.
